logging.c(3) Library Functions Manual logging.c(3)

NAME

logging.c - Logging category options.

SYNOPSIS

Variables


const registry_entry_t g_logging_entries []

Detailed Description

Logging category options.

Binary-level logging and output control options including log files, verbosity levels, and grep filtering.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Definition in file options/registry/logging.c.

Variable Documentation

const registry_entry_t g_logging_entries[]

Definition at line 21 of file options/registry/logging.c.

21                                            {
22     // LOGGING GROUP (binary-level)
23     {"log-file",
24      'L',
25      OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK,
26      offsetof(options_t, log_file),
27      NULL, // Use mode_default_getter instead
28      0,
29 #ifndef NDEBUG
30      "Set FILE as path for log file (default: current directory; filenames: server.log, client.log, mirror.log, "
31      "acds.log, discovery.log).",
32 #else
33      "Set FILE as path for log file (default: $TMPDIR/ascii-chat/ or %TEMP%\\ascii-chat\\; filenames: server.log, "
34      "client.log, mirror.log, acds.log, discovery.log).",
35 #endif
36      "LOGGING",
37      "FILE",
38      false,
39      "ASCII_CHAT_LOG_FILE",
40      NULL,
41      parse_log_file,
42      false,
43      false,
44 #ifndef NDEBUG
45      OPTION_MODE_CLIENT | OPTION_MODE_SERVER | OPTION_MODE_MIRROR | OPTION_MODE_DISCOVERY | OPTION_MODE_DISCOVERY_SVC,
46 #else
47      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
48 #endif
49      {0},
50      get_default_log_file},
51     {"log-level",
52      '\0',
53      OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK,
54      offsetof(options_t, log_level),
55      &default_log_level_value,
56      sizeof(log_level_t),
57      "Set log level: dev, debug, info, warn, error, fatal. Logs at or above this level are written.",
58      "LOGGING",
59      NULL,
60      false,
61      "ASCII_CHAT_LOG_LEVEL",
62      NULL,
63      parse_log_level,
64      false,
65      false,
66      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
67      {.enum_values = g_log_level_values,
68       .enum_descriptions = g_log_level_descs,
69       .enum_integer_values = g_log_level_integers,
70       .input_type = OPTION_INPUT_ENUM},
71      NULL},
72     {"verbose",
73      'V',
74      OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK,
75      offsetof(options_t, verbose_level),
76      0,
77      sizeof(unsigned short int),
78      "Increase log verbosity (stackable: -VV, -VVV).",
79      "LOGGING",
80      NULL,
81      false,
82      "ASCII_CHAT_VERBOSE",
83      NULL,
84      parse_verbose_flag,
85      false,
86      true,
87      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
88      {0},
89      NULL},
90     {"quiet",
91      'q',
92      OPTION_TYPE_BOOL,
93      offsetof(options_t, quiet),
94      &default_quiet_value,
95      sizeof(bool),
96      "Disable console logging (log to file only).",
97      "LOGGING",
98      NULL,
99      false,
100      "ASCII_CHAT_QUIET",
101      NULL,
102      NULL,
103      false,
104      false,
105      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
106      {0},
107      NULL},
108     {"grep",
109      '\0',
110      OPTION_TYPE_STRING,
111      offsetof(options_t, grep_pattern),
112      NULL, // Default is empty string - not meaningful to display in help
113      sizeof(char *),
114      "Filter console logs with perl compatible regular expressions or fixed strings. "
115      "Format 1: /pattern/flags (with flags). Format 2: pattern (plain regex, no flags). "
116      "Flags (format 1 only): i(case-insensitive), m(multiline), s(dotall), x(extended), g(global highlight), "
117      "I(invert match), F(fixed string), A<n>(n lines after), B<n>(n lines before), C<n>(n lines of context). "
118      "Examples: '/error/i', 'error', '/panic/C5', 'warn|error', '/buffer pool/Fg'. Multiple --grep allowed (OR).",
119      "LOGGING",
120      "PATTERN",
121      false,
122      "ASCII_CHAT_GREP",
123      NULL,
124      NULL,
125      false,
126      false,
127      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
128      {.examples = (const char *[]){"ERROR", "client\\.c", "network.*failed", NULL}},
129      NULL},
130     {"color-scheme",
131      '\0',
132      OPTION_TYPE_STRING,
133      offsetof(options_t, color_scheme_name),
134      OPT_COLOR_SCHEME_NAME_DEFAULT,
135      sizeof(((options_t *)0)->color_scheme_name),
136      "Color scheme for logging output. Built-in schemes: pastel, nord, solarized, dracula, gruvbox, "
137      "monokai, base16-default. All schemes with light variants auto-adapt to terminal background.",
138      "LOGGING",
139      "NAME",
140      false,
141      "ASCII_CHAT_COLOR_SCHEME",
142      NULL,
143      NULL,
144      false,
145      false,
146      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
147      {.input_type = OPTION_INPUT_STRING},
148      NULL},
149     {"json",
150      '\0',
151      OPTION_TYPE_BOOL,
152      offsetof(options_t, json),
153      &default_json_value,
154      sizeof(bool),
155      "Enable JSON logging (machine-readable NDJSON format). Disabled by default (text format).",
156      "LOGGING",
157      NULL,
158      false,
159      "ASCII_CHAT_JSON",
160      NULL,
161      NULL,
162      false,
163      false,
164      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
165      {0},
166      NULL},
167     {"log-template",
168      '\0',
169      OPTION_TYPE_STRING,
170      offsetof(options_t, log_template),
171      NULL,
172      sizeof(((options_t *)0)->log_template),
173      "Custom log format string. Format specifiers: %time(fmt) - time with strftime format (see 'man 3 strftime' for "
174      "codes like %Y, %m, %d, %H, %M, %S); "
175      "%level - log level (DEV/DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR/FATAL); %level_aligned - level padded to 5 chars; "
176      "%file - full file path; %file_relative - file path relative to project root; %line - line number; "
177      "%func - function name; %tid - thread ID; %message - log message; "
178      "%color(LEVEL, text) - colorize text using LEVEL's color from current scheme (e.g., %color(INFO, %tid)). "
179      "Example: '[%time(%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S)] [%level_aligned] %file_relative:%line %message'. "
180      "Escape %% for literal %, \\\\ for literal backslash. "
181      "Default: release mode '[%time(%H:%M:%S)] [%level_aligned] %message' or debug mode '[%time(%H:%M:%S)] "
182      "[%level_aligned] [tid:%tid] %file_relative:%line in %func(): %message'.",
183      "LOGGING",
184      "TEMPLATE",
185      false,
186      "ASCII_CHAT_LOG_TEMPLATE",
187      NULL,
188      NULL,
189      false,
190      false,
191      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
192      {.input_type = OPTION_INPUT_STRING},
193      NULL},
194     {"log-format-console",
195      '\0',
196      OPTION_TYPE_BOOL,
197      offsetof(options_t, log_format_console_only),
198      NULL,
199      sizeof(bool),
200      "Apply custom log template (--log-template) only to console output (file logs use default format). See "
201      "--log-template "
202      "for supported format specifiers.",
203      "LOGGING",
204      NULL,
205      false,
206      "ASCII_CHAT_LOG_FORMAT_CONSOLE",
207      NULL,
208      NULL,
209      false,
210      false,
211      OPTION_MODE_BINARY,
212      {0},
213      NULL},
214 
215     REGISTRY_TERMINATOR()};
